Title: Gas (Market Transparency) Amendment Bill
Description: This bill amends the Gas Act 1992, and is intended to improve the information available to help the Government monitor gas markets and help market participants make more efficient decisions.

Associations.ItemDisplayName: 634 - Gas (Market Transparency) Amendment Bill

Title: Credit Contracts and Consumer Finance Amendment Bill
Act: Credit Contracts and Consumer Finance Amendment Act 2026
Description: This bill aims to simplify and streamline the effective regulation of financial services by aligning both the regulator and aspects of the regulatory model for consumer credit with that under the Financial Markets Conduct Act 2013, as well as making other improvements to ensure the legislation is clear and proportionate.

Associations.ItemDisplayName: Credit Contracts and Consumer Finance Amendment Bill
505 - Credit Contracts and Consumer Finance Amendment Bill

Title: Social Security (Modernisation) Amendment Bill
Description: This bill amends the Social Security Act 2018 and the Social Security Regulation 2018, and is intended to enable the administration of a more efficient, modern welfare system through the use of automated decision making.

Title: Taxation (Budget Measures) Bill (No 3)
Description: This bill amends the Income Tax Act 2007, the Student Loan Scheme Act 2011, and the Tax Administration Act 1994, and contains tax measures announced as part of Budget 2026.

Title: Patents Amendment Bill
Description: This bill amends the Patents Act 2013 to apply stricter criteria for the grant of divisional applications filed under the Patents Act 1953, so that 1953 Act applications are examined in broadly the same way as 2013 Act applications.

Associations.ItemDisplayName: Patents Amendment Bill

Title: Regulatory Systems (Internal Affairs) Amendment Bill
Description: This overall policy objective of this omnibus bill is to improve the effectiveness and efficiency of regulatory systems overseen by the Department of Internal Affairs.

Associations.ItemDisplayName: Regulatory Systems (Internal Affairs) Amendment Bill

Title: Financial Service Providers (Registration and Dispute Resolution) Amendment Bill
Act: Financial Service Providers (Registration and Dispute Resolution) Amendment Act 2026
Description: This bill aims to ensure that financial dispute resolution schemes are governed and operated in an effective and independent manner for the benefit of consumers.

Associations.ItemDisplayName: Financial Service Providers (Registration and Dispute Resolution) Amendment Bill

Title: Building and Construction Sector (Self-certification by Plumbers and Drainlayers) Amendment Bill
Act: Building and Construction Sector (Self-certification by Plumbers and Drainlayers) Amendment Act 2026
Description: The key purpose of this omnibus bill is to speed up the consenting process for, and building of, houses in New Zealand by reducing the number of inspections that must be completed for plumbing and drainlaying work.

Associations.ItemDisplayName: 632 - Building and Construction Sector (Self-certification by Plumbers and Drainlayers) Amendment Bill
578 - Building and Construction Sector (Self-certification by Plumbers and Drainlayers) Amendment Bill
575 - Building and Construction Sector (Self-certification by Plumbers and Drainlayers) Amendment Bill
576 - Building and Construction Sector (Self-certification by Plumbers and Drainlayers) Amendment Bill
Building and Construction Sector (Self-certification by Plumbers and Drainlayers) Amendment Bill

Title: Public Service Amendment Bill
Description: This bill amends the Public Service Act 2020 with the intention to drive improvements in public service performance, create more stability, and ensure that the public service efficiently and effectively delivers value for money.

Associations.ItemDisplayName: Public Service Amendment Bill

Title: Environment (Disestablishment of Ministry for the Environment) Amendment Bill
Act: Environment (Disestablishment of Ministry for the Environment) Amendment Act 2026
Description: This bill disestablishes the Ministry for the Environment. The Government intends to consolidate the functions of the ministry, along with the Ministry of Housing and Urban Development, the Ministry of Transport, and the local government functions of the Department of Internal Affairs, into a new ministry.

Associations.ItemDisplayName: Environment (Disestablishment of Ministry for the Environment) Amendment Bill

Title: Appropriation (2025/26 Supplementary Estimates) Bill
Description: This bill seeks parliamentary authorisation of the individual appropriations and changes to individual appropriations contained in The Supplementary Estimates of Appropriations for the Government of New Zealand for the Year Ending 30 June 2026.

Title: Appropriation (2026/27 Estimates) Bill
Description: This bill seeks parliamentary authorisation of the individual appropriations contained in The Estimates of Appropriations for the Government of New Zealand for the Year Ending 30 June 2027.

Title: Fair Trading Amendment Bill
Description: The objectives of this bill are to ensure that the Fair Trading Act 1986 remains fit for purpose as business practices and digital markets evolve, strengthen deterrence of unfair trading conduct, support appropriate consequences for breaches, and enable efficient and effective enforcement.

Title: Disability Support Services Bill
Description: This bill provides a new foundational legislative framework to strengthen and stabilise the provision of disability support services.

Title: Summary Offences (Move-on Orders) Amendment Bill
Description: This bill amends the Summary Offences Act 1981 to empower the New Zealand Police to issue move-on orders to manage low-level disorderly behaviour and prevent escalation. The amendments aim to ensure that the Police are adequately equipped to address public disorder, increase the public’s sense of safety, and support urban centres and economies.

Associations.ItemDisplayName: Report of the Attorney-General under the New Zealand Bill of Rights Act 1990 on the Summary Offences (Move-on Orders) Amendment Bill

Title: Crimes (Impeding Major Bridges, Tunnels, and Roads) Amendment Bill

ProposedMembersBillInfo.Title: Crimes (Impeding Major Bridges, Tunnels, and Roads) Amendment Bill

Description: This bill amends the Crimes Act 1961 to create a new offence for damaging or obstructing a major road, tunnel, or bridge.

Associations.ItemDisplayName: Crimes (Impeding Major Bridges, Tunnels, and Roads) Amendment Bill

Title: New Zealand Humanitarian Aid and Disaster Relief Medal Bill

ProposedMembersBillInfo.Title: New Zealand Humanitarian Aid and Disaster Relief Medal Bill

Description: This bill provides for a new service medal, the New Zealand Humanitarian Aid and Disaster Relief Medal, to recognise service undertaken in the context of humanitarian aid or disaster relief activities on behalf of New Zealand.

Associations.ItemDisplayName: New Zealand Humanitarian Aid and Disaster Relief Medal Bill

Title: Local Government (Management of Local Authorities) Amendment Bill

ProposedMembersBillInfo.Title: Local Government (Management of Local Authorities) Amendment Bill

Description: The purpose of this bill is to provide the mayor and elected councilors with the mandate to more effectively govern and control the activities of their local authority.

Associations.ItemDisplayName: Local Government (Management of Local Authorities) Amendment Bill

Title: Oranga Tamariki (Suitable Accommodation on Leaving Care) Amendment Bill

ProposedMembersBillInfo.Title: Oranga Tamariki (Suitable Accommodation on Leaving Care) Amendment Bill

Description: This bill amends the Oranga Tamariki Act 1989 to make the chief executive of Oranga Tamariki responsible for providing all support required to arrange suitable accommodation for young people exiting care.

Associations.ItemDisplayName: Oranga Tamariki (Suitable Accommodation on Leaving Care) Amendment Bill

Title: Crown Minerals (Prohibition on Mining on Conservation Land) Amendment Bill

ProposedMembersBillInfo.Title: Crown Minerals (Prohibition on Mining on Conservation Land) Amendment Bill

Description: This bill prohibits new exploration, prospecting, and mining activity on conservation lands and waters.

Associations.ItemDisplayName: Crown Minerals (Prohibition on Mining on Conservation Land) Amendment Bill

Title: Residential Tenancies (Requiring Landlords to Provide Curtains) Amendment Bill

ProposedMembersBillInfo.Title: Residential Tenancies (Requiring Landlords to Provide Curtains) Amendment Bill

Description: This bill amends the Residential Tenancies Act 1986 so that the healthy homes standards must include requirements for landlords to provide, where practicable, insulated curtains, insulated blinds, or equivalents.

Associations.ItemDisplayName: Residential Tenancies (Requiring Landlords to Provide Curtains) Amendment Bill

Title: Legislation (Definitions of Woman and Man) Amendment Bill

ProposedMembersBillInfo.Title: Legislation (Definitions of Woman and Man) Amendment Bill

Description: This bill seeks to ensure clarity and consistency in New Zealand law by defining "woman" as "an adult human biological female" and "man" as "an adult human biological male".

Associations.ItemDisplayName: Report of the Attorney-General under the New Zealand Bill of Rights Act 1990 on the Legislation (Definitions of Woman and Man) Amendment Bill
Legislation (Definitions of Woman and Man) Amendment Bill

Title: Deepfake Digital Harm and Exploitation Bill

ProposedMembersBillInfo.Title: Deepfake Digital Harm and Exploitation Bill

Description: This bill amends the Crimes Act 1961 and the Harmful Digital Communications Act 2015 to expand the definition of an "intimate visual recording" to explicitly include images created, synthesised, or altered to show a person’s likeness produced without consent.

Associations.ItemDisplayName: Deepfake Digital Harm and Exploitation Bill

Title: Local Government (Port Companies Accountability) Amendment Bill

ProposedMembersBillInfo.Title: Local Government (Port Companies Accountability) Amendment Bill

Description: This bill reverses the exclusion of port companies and their subsidiaries from certain rules that apply to council-controlled organisations in the Local Government Act 2002.

Associations.ItemDisplayName: Local Government (Port Companies Accountability) Amendment Bill

Title: Public Finance (Prohibition on Providing Public Funds to Gangs) Amendment Bill

ProposedMembersBillInfo.Title: Public Finance (Prohibition on Providing Public Funds to Gangs) Amendment Bill

Description: This bill would prohibit the Crown and its agencies from providing funds directly and indirectly to organisations that are run, administered, or associated to gangs.

Associations.ItemDisplayName: Public Finance (Prohibition on Providing Public Funds to Gangs) Amendment Bill

Title: Better Regional Boundaries Bill
Description: The purpose of this bill is to ensure that, within a reasonable time-frame of 5 years, each government entity aligns boundaries and aligns them with local authority boundaries.

Associations.ItemDisplayName: Better Regional Boundaries Bill

Title: Concealment of Location of Victim Remains Bill

ProposedMembersBillInfo.Title: Concealment of Location of Victim Remains Bill

Description: This bill provides that offenders who refuse to disclose the location of a victim's body may be denied parole.

Associations.ItemDisplayName: Concealment of Location of Victim Remains Bill

Title: Social Security (Jobseeker Support and Accommodation Supplement) Amendment Bill
Description: This bill amends the Social Security Act 2018 to support fiscal sustainability in the welfare system by better targeting financial assistance to those most in need of support. It tightens eligibility criteria for jobseeker support and the equivalent emergency benefit for young people and targets the accommodation supplement more tightly to some homeowners.

Title: Education and Training (System Reform) Amendment Bill
Description: This bill amends the Education and Training Act 2020  to give effect to new policy decisions and to make other minor and technical changes.

Associations.ItemDisplayName: 584 - Education and Training (System Reform) Amendment Bill

Title: Redress System for Abuse in Care Bill
Description: The purpose of this bill is to introduce a presumption against financial redress for serious violent or sexual offenders and provide legal protections for core State agencies when making apologies for abuse in State care.

Associations.ItemDisplayName: Redress System for Abuse in Care Bill
582 - Redress System for Abuse in Care Bill
581 - Redress System for Abuse in Care Bill
569 - Redress System for Abuse in Care Bill

Title: Anti-Money Laundering and Countering Financing of Terrorism (Supervisor, Levy, and Other Matters) Amendment Bill
Act: Anti-Money Laundering and Countering Financing of Terrorism (Supervisor, Levy, and Other Matters) Amendment Act 2026
Description: The objectives of this bill are, reducing business compliance costs; improving regulatory effectiveness and efficiency; continued regulatory compliance with related international obligations; and providing sufficient funding for the regulatory system.

Associations.ItemDisplayName: Anti-Money Laundering and Countering Financing of Terrorism (Supervisor, Levy, and Other Matters) Amendment Bill
573 - Anti-Money Laundering and Countering Financing of Terrorism (Supervisor, Levy, and Other Matters) Amendment Bill

Title: Ngāti Rāhiri Tumutumu Claims Settlement Bill
Act: Ngāti Rāhiri Tumutumu Claims Settlement Act 2026
Description: This bill records the acknowledgements and apology made to Ngāti Rāhiri Tumutumu by the Crown when the relevant deed of settlement was signed and gives effect to redress in the deed that requires legislation.

Associations.ItemDisplayName: Ngāti Rāhiri Tumutumu Claims Settlement Bill

Title: Regulatory Systems (Transport) Amendment Bill
Description: This bill is an omnibus bill that amends land transport, maritime, and aviation legislation.

Associations.ItemDisplayName: 574 - Regulatory Systems (Transport) Amendment Bill
Regulatory Systems (Transport) Amendment Bill

Title: Health Practitioners Competence Assurance Amendment Bill
Description: This bill seeks to better align health workforce regulation with patient needs, health system policy, and Government priorities. It also seeks to ensure that regulation is responsive to support innovative health services and ensure efficient recognition and registration of health practitioners.

Title: Ngāti Tara Tokanui Claims Settlement Bill
Act: Ngāti Tara Tokanui Claims Settlement Act 2026
Description: This settlement bill will record in English and te reo Māori the acknowledgements and apology given by the Crown to Ngāti Tara Tokanui in the deed of settlement, signed on 28 July 2022, and gives effect to certain provisions of the deed of settlement that settle the historical claims of Ngāti Tara Tokanui.

Associations.ItemDisplayName: 570 - Ngāti Tara Tokanui Claims Settlement Bill
Ngāti Tara Tokanui Claims Settlement Bill

Title: Anti-Money Laundering and Countering Financing of Terrorism Amendment Bill
Act: Anti-Money Laundering and Countering Financing of Terrorism Amendment Act 2026
Description: This bill is included in a package of bills amending regulatory systems administered by the Ministry of Justice. It aims to improve New Zealand's anti-money laundering and terrorism financing regime to make the system more risk-based, efficient, and effective.

Associations.ItemDisplayName: 572 - Anti-Money Laundering and Countering Financing of Terrorism Amendment Bill
Anti-Money Laundering and Countering Financing of Terrorism Amendment Bill

Title: Ngāti Hei Claims Settlement Bill
Description: This bill gives effect to some elements of the Ngāti Hei deed of settlement, which was signed on 17 August 2017 by Ngāti Hei and the Crown. This bill records the acknowledgements and apology made by the Crown to iwi when the deed was signed and gives effect to redress in the deed.

Associations.ItemDisplayName: 577 - Ngāti Hei Claims Settlement Bill
Ngāti Hei Claims Settlement Bill

Title: Agricultural Compounds and Veterinary Medicines Amendment Bill
Description: This bill amends the Agricultural Compounds and Veterinary Medicines Act 1997 to give effect to the recommendations of the Agricultural and Horticultural Products Regulatory Review that relate to the principal Act.

Title: Hazardous Substances and New Organisms Amendment Bill
Description: This bill amends the Hazardous Substances and New Organisms Act 1996 to streamline and improve application processes and pathways, support risk-proportionate decision-making and public participation, and enhance compliance, monitoring, and enforcement under the Act.

Title: Ngā Hapū o Te Iwi o Whanganui Claims Settlement Bill
Description: This bill seeks to give effect to certain matters set out in the Ngā Hapū o Te Iwi o Whanganui Deed of Settlement, signed on 2 May 2026.

Title: Overseas Adoptions Legislation Bill
Description: This bill seeks to establish an enduring system for international and overseas adoptions that supports the wellbeing and best interests of adopted persons, aligns with New Zealand's international obligations, and upholds the integrity of New Zealand's immigration and citizenship systems.

Title: Conservation Amendment Bill
Description: This bill amends the Conservation Act 1987 to streamline the management of land, better enable appropriate use of conservation land, and support more effective administration of conservation land.

Title: Local Government (Auckland Council) (Transport Governance) Amendment Bill
Act: Local Government (Auckland Council) (Transport Governance) Amendment Act 2026
Description: This bill is intended to improve the transport system in Auckland by establishing the Auckland Regional Transport Committee to undertake strategic transport planning, transferring most transport functions from Auckland Transport to Auckland Council, allocating decision making for local transport functions to Auckland local boards, and reforming the role and functions of Auckland Transport.

Associations.ItemDisplayName: 568 - Local Government (Auckland Council) (Transport Governance) Amendment Bill
564 - Local Government (Auckland Council) (Transport Governance) Amendment Bill
Local Government (Auckland Council) (Transport Governance) Amendment Bill

Title: Carter Trust Amendment Bill
Description: This bill seeks to wind up the Carter Trust, established under the will of the late Charles Rooking Carter, and enable Public Trust to make distributions as executor of the will.

Associations.ItemDisplayName: Carter Trust Amendment Bill

Title: Serious Fraud Office Amendment Bill
Description: This bill amends the Serious Fraud Office Act 1990 to ensure that the Serious Fraud Office can more effectively carry out its role by updating provisions relating to search warrants and evidence admissibility.

Title: Crimes (Virginity Testing Practices) Amendment Bill

Associations.ItemDisplayName: Crimes (Virginity Testing Practices) Amendment Bill

Title: Criminal Records (Clean Slate) (Additional Eligibility) Amendment Bill

ProposedMembersBillInfo.Title: Criminal Records (Clean Slate) (Additional Eligibility) Amendment Bill

Description: This bill expands the class of people who are eligible for the clean slate scheme with regard to their criminal record after 10 years of lawful behaviour.

Associations.ItemDisplayName: Criminal Records (Clean Slate) (Additional Eligibility) Amendment Bill

Title: Financial Markets (International Money Transfers) Amendment Bill

ProposedMembersBillInfo.Title: Financial Markets (International Money Transfers) Amendment Bill

Description: This bill will amend the Financial Markets Conduct Act 2013 to require greater transparency for fees charged on international money transfer services.

Associations.ItemDisplayName: Financial Markets (International Money Transfers) Amendment Bill

Title: Residential Tenancies (Registration of Boarding House Landlords) Amendment Bill

ProposedMembersBillInfo.Title: Residential Tenancies (Registration of Boarding House Landlords) Amendment Bill

Description: This bill makes changes to the Residential Tenancies Act 1986 to require the Ministry of Housing and Urban Development to create and maintain a register of boarding houses and boarding house landlords to better monitor compliance with residential tenancy laws.

Associations.ItemDisplayName: Residential Tenancies (Registration of Boarding House Landlords) Amendment Bill

Title: Modern Slavery Bill
Description: The purpose of this jointly sponsored bill is to implement a statutory framework to strengthen and coordinate actions to combat modern slavery.

Associations.ItemDisplayName: Modern Slavery Bill

Title: Drug Overdose (Assistance Protection) Legislation Bill
Description: The bill is intended to ensure that people who call for help when they are with someone who is experiencing a drug overdose or adverse reaction are protected from being charged for low-level drug offences under the Misuse of Drugs Act 1975.

Title: Corrections (Management of Prisoners, and Prisoners’ Property) Amendment Bill
Description: This bill amends the Corrections Act 2004. Its most substantive amendments would establish a clearer statutory basis for the safe, lawful, and humane management of a small group of prisoners who pose an extreme risk that the Department of Corrections manages in prison.

Title: Online Casino Gambling Bill
Description: This bill establishes a licensing regime for online casino gambling to facilitate a safer and regulated online casino gambling market.
